rules.name: 4-layer dependency direction
rules.check: Dependencies flow inward: Api → Migrations → Application → Domain. Flag reverse imports.

rules.name: Feature-based CQRS folders
rules.check: {Feature}/Commands/ and {Feature}/Queries/ structure. Flag misplaced files.

rules.name: Co-locate command+handler+DTO
rules.check: IRequest<T> and its IRequestHandler in same file. Shared DTOs in DTOs/ subfolder.

rules.name: Shared helpers in feature files
rules.check: Flag >10 line duplication across handlers in same feature. Don't flag small duplications.

rules.name: Direct DbContext in handlers accepted
rules.check: Do NOT flag DbContext injection. Flag generic repository pattern introduction.

rules.name: All entities inherit BaseEntity
rules.check: Flag entities not inheriting BaseEntity. Flag redefined Id/audit fields.

rules.name: Rich domain model
rules.check: Business logic (state transitions, validation) belongs on entity. Flag handlers directly mutating complex state.

rules.name: Enums stored as strings
rules.check: Verify HasConversion<string>() for enums. Flag integer storage.

rules.name: Explicit foreign keys
rules.check: Every navigation property needs corresponding FK property.

rules.name: DeleteBehavior.Restrict default
rules.check: Flag Cascade/SetNull without justification comment.

rules.name: Suggest value objects
rules.check: Flag raw string for email/token/branch on new/modified code. Suggest value object.

rules.name: Primary constructors
rules.check: Flag traditional constructors with field assignments.

rules.name: Unit return for side-effect commands
rules.check: Do NOT flag Unit returns. Flag IActionResult/ActionResult returns from handlers.

rules.name: Primitive returns acceptable
rules.check: Do NOT flag Guid/string/int/bool returns. Flag IActionResult/object.
rules.why: Single value doesn't need DTO wrapping.

rules.name: Handler orchestration OK if simple
rules.check: Do NOT flag clear sequential multi-op handlers. Flag 3+ nesting levels.

rules.name: Commands may read DB
rules.check: Do NOT flag database reads in command handlers.

rules.name: Pipeline behaviors for repeated patterns
rules.check: Suggest pipeline behavior when same boilerplate in 3+ handlers.

rules.name: Create commands return Guid only
rules.check: Create command handlers (IRequest<T> for POST endpoints) must return Guid (the created entity's Id), not a full DTO. Flag IRequest<SomeDto> on create commands. Callers must query separately if they need the full entity.
rules.why: Create endpoints should return the minimum needed (the Id). Returning full DTOs couples the write path to the read model and fetches data the caller may not need.

rules.name: AsNoTracking for read-only queries
rules.check: Query handlers (no SaveChangesAsync) must use .AsNoTracking().

rules.name: Separate IEntityTypeConfiguration
rules.check: OnModelCreating only calls ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly. Flag inline Entity<T>() calls.

rules.name: JSONB hybrid config
rules.check: Typed C# classes: OwnsOne/.ToJson(). Dictionary/JsonDocument: HasColumnType('jsonb') OK.

rules.name: Flag raw SQL
rules.check: Flag FromSqlRaw/ExecuteSqlRaw/Interpolated. Higher severity for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

rules.name: Descriptive migration names
rules.check: Flag generic names (Migration1, Update, Fix). Verify Down reverses Up.

rules.name: Audit interceptor not bypassed
rules.check: Flag ExecuteSql INSERT/UPDATE. Flag direct ADO.NET writes. Flag interceptor removal.

rules.name: Remove unnecessary Includes
rules.check: Every .Include() must be used downstream. Flag unused includes.

rules.name: Bounded queries on large tables
rules.check: Flag unbounded ToListAsync on Candidates/Invites/Evaluations/GitEvents.

rules.name: No explicit Id assignment for BaseEntity
rules.check: Flag 'Id = Guid.CreateVersion7()', 'Id = Guid.NewGuid()', or any explicit Id assignment when creating entities that inherit BaseEntity. EF Core ValueGeneratedOnAdd handles Id generation.
rules.why: Redundant code; EF Core auto-generates Guid PKs via ValueGeneratedOnAdd. Explicit assignment bypasses the database generation strategy.

rules.name: Use navigation properties for child entity creation
rules.check: When creating a parent entity with children, assign children via the navigation collection property (e.g., Stacks = [..items]). Flag manual FK assignment (e.g., ChallengeId = parent.Id) followed by separate db.ChildSet.Add(). Flag adding child entities to their own DbSet instead of the parent's navigation property.
rules.why: EF Core automatically resolves FKs through navigation properties. Manual FK + separate Add is redundant and error-prone.

rules.name: Fail-fast validation at top
rules.check: All validation before any mutation or side effect.

rules.name: ApiException hierarchy only
rules.check: Flag throw new Exception/InvalidOperationException/ArgumentException for business errors.

rules.name: ProblemDetails format
rules.check: Flag hardcoded English in exception constructors. New exceptions must extend ApiException.

rules.name: Null handling on entity lookups
rules.check: Flag OrDefault without null check. Flag SingleAsync where not-found should 404.

rules.name: No swallowed exceptions
rules.check: Flag empty catch. Flag catch without rethrow/log/convert.

rules.name: Log external service failures
rules.check: Catch blocks around HTTP/SMTP/GitHub must log at Warning/Error.

rules.name: ProblemDetails custom error codes
check: Flag ApiException constructor calls with inline string code parameter (must use ErrorCodes.*)
Flag error responses with translated/localized text in detail (must be English-only)
Flag controllers that manually construct error JSON instead of throwing ApiException
rules.why: Backend is language-agnostic. Translations are frontend's responsibility. Custom codes provide a stable contract. Inline strings drift.
rules.fix: Use ErrorCodes.* constants. Let frontend map codes via errors.codes.{code}.

rules.name: Thin controllers
rules.check: Only mediator.Send(). Flag business logic, DB access, service calls in controllers.

rules.name: Consistent HTTP status codes
rules.check: All endpoints return 200 with concrete types (per BE-API-007). POST create returns the Guid directly. PUT/PATCH returns the updated DTO or Unit. DELETE returns Unit. Do NOT use CreatedAtAction or StatusCode(201) — just return the value and let ASP.NET default to 200.
rules.why: Concrete return types with default 200 status are simpler and produce better OpenAPI specs. The frontend doesn't check status codes.

rules.name: Route naming conventions
rules.check: Lowercase kebab-case plural nouns. Flag uppercase/camelCase/underscores/singular.

rules.name: AllowAnonymous requires comment
rules.check: Flag [AllowAnonymous] without justification comment.

rules.name: OpenAPI auto-generation sufficient
rules.check: Do NOT flag missing [ProducesResponseType]. Concrete return types (per BE-API-007) already produce accurate specs.

rules.name: No API versioning
rules.check: Flag /v1/, /v2/, api-version prefixes.

rules.name: No ActionResult return types
rules.check: Flag ActionResult, ActionResult<T>, and IActionResult as controller method return types. Controllers must return the concrete type directly (e.g., Task<Guid>, Task<MyDto>, Task<List<MyDto>>). For non-200 status codes, use StatusCode() helper which returns ObjectResult. Error responses are handled by the ApiException hierarchy and ProblemDetails middleware — no need for ActionResult wrappers.
rules.why: ActionResult<T> is a leaky abstraction that adds no value when errors are handled via exceptions. Concrete return types produce accurate OpenAPI specs without [ProducesResponseType] annotations.

rules.name: EndpointName attribute over Name parameter
rules.check: Endpoint names must be declared via a separate [EndpointName("...")]  attribute, NOT via the Name parameter inside HTTP method attributes (e.g., [HttpGet(Name = "...")], [HttpPost("route", Name = "...")]). Flag any Http{Verb} attribute that contains a Name parameter.
rules.why: Separating the endpoint name from the route template keeps each attribute single-purpose and makes endpoint names easier to search, rename, and review independently of routing.

rules.name: No redundant route type constraints
rules.check: Flag route constraints that duplicate the method parameter type. E.g., flag {id:guid} when the parameter is already typed as Guid id. Use plain {id} instead. The C# parameter type already enforces the type via model binding.
rules.why: Redundant — the method signature already declares the type. Duplicating it in the route adds noise and maintenance burden.

rules.name: Group related controllers in subfolders
rules.check: When a domain area has 2+ controllers (e.g., CompaniesController + CompanyInvitesController, or AssessmentsController + AssessmentTemplatesController), they must live in a subfolder under Controllers/ named with the plural domain noun (e.g., Controllers/Companies/, Controllers/Assessments/). Flag multiple controllers sharing the same domain prefix that sit flat in Controllers/.
rules.why: Flat controller directories become unnavigable as the API grows. Grouping by domain keeps related endpoints co-located and discoverable.

rules.name: No hardcoded secrets
rules.check: Flag long alphanumeric strings, ghp_*, eyJ*, Password= in connection strings, secret variable literals.

rules.name: Centralized token validation
rules.check: Candidate endpoints must use centralized validation. Flag duplicated token logic.

rules.name: Identity through ICurrentUser only
rules.check: Flag HttpContext.User, User.FindFirst, ClaimsPrincipal outside ICurrentUser impl.

rules.name: No sensitive data in prod logs
rules.check: Flag candidate emails/tokens in LogInformation/Warning/Error.

rules.name: No secrets in logs at any level
rules.check: Flag variables named pat/token/secret/password/apiKey in log templates.

rules.name: Authorization via policies and attributes, never path-matching middleware
rules.check: Flag any middleware that makes authorization decisions based on URL path strings (StartsWithSegments, path.Contains, path.Equals, route prefix whitelists). Authorization bypass/enforcement MUST use ASP.NET authorization policies with endpoint metadata (attributes). Flag HashSet<string> of whitelisted paths/prefixes used for auth decisions.
rules.why: Path-matching middleware silently breaks on route renames — no compiler error, no test failure. The security posture of an endpoint becomes split across files and invisible during code review. Attributes are compiler-enforced, co-located with the endpoint, precise (no glob/prefix collisions), and integrate with ASP.NET's authorization pipeline (Swagger, endpoint metadata, integration tests).

how.pattern: Define an IAuthorizationRequirement + IAuthorizationHandler that checks endpoint metadata for a bypass attribute. Register as a named policy or fallback policy. Apply [BypassX] on specific actions/controllers that should skip the check. Default behavior is fail-closed (no attribute = check enforced).
dos: Use IAuthorizationRequirement + IAuthorizationHandler for cross-cutting auth checks like 'must have companyId claim'
Use a marker attribute (e.g., [BypassCompanyRequired]) for endpoints that should skip the check
Check for the marker attribute via endpoint metadata inside the handler: context.Resource is HttpContext http && http.GetEndpoint()?.Metadata.GetMetadata<BypassCompanyRequiredAttribute>() is not null
Register the policy via AddAuthorizationBuilder().AddPolicy(...) or set it as FallbackPolicy for automatic enforcement on all authenticated endpoints
Place the bypass attribute directly on the action method or controller class — it must be visible where the endpoint is defined
Prefer fail-closed defaults: absence of the bypass attribute means the check is enforced
When using FallbackPolicy, remember it only applies to endpoints that have no other [Authorize] or [AllowAnonymous] — combine with RequireAuthorization() on the endpoint group if needed
donts: NEVER make authorization decisions by matching request path strings (StartsWithSegments, Contains, Equals, regex) in middleware
NEVER maintain a HashSet/List of whitelisted URL prefixes or paths for auth bypass
NEVER split the security posture of an endpoint across a middleware file and the controller — keep it co-located via attributes

NEVER use middleware for checks that depend on 'which endpoint is being called' — middleware runs before endpoint routing resolves in some configurations and cannot see endpoint metadata reliably; use IAuthorizationHandler which runs after routing
NEVER rely on convention/prefix matching like 'all /api/auth/* endpoints skip this check' — a future /api/auth-logs controller would accidentally be whitelisted
Do not use [AllowAnonymous] as the bypass mechanism for authenticated-but-missing-claim scenarios — it skips ALL authorization, not just the company check. Use a dedicated marker attribute.
how.example_before:
private static readonly HashSet<string> WhitelistedPrefixes = ["/api/auth", "/api/admin"];
if (context.Request.Path.StartsWithSegments(prefix)) { await next(context); return; }

rules.name: Transitions via entity methods
rules.check: Flag direct status assignment in handlers. Must use entity methods (MarkAsSent(), etc).

rules.name: Transitions audit-logged
rules.check: Transition methods must capture old state before changing.

rules.name: Terminal states immutable
rules.check: Check terminal state before mutation. Invites: Completed/Expired/Revoked. Evaluations: Completed/Failed.

rules.name: Evaluation state machine rigor
rules.check: Evaluation needs explicit methods (MarkInProgress, Complete, Fail), validation, terminal checks.

rules.name: State machines documented
rules.check: Entities with status enums must document valid transitions in comment.

rules.name: Error handling in loops
rules.check: ExecuteAsync per-iteration try/catch. Flag unhandled exceptions exiting loop.
rules.why: One bad record can't block all.

rules.name: Config-driven intervals
rules.check: Flag hardcoded time literals. Must come from IOptions<T>/IConfiguration.

rules.name: Graceful shutdown
rules.check: stoppingToken in loop condition, Task.Delay, and all async ops.

rules.name: Bulk ops via EF Core 7+
rules.check: Flag load-iterate-save when ExecuteUpdateAsync/ExecuteDeleteAsync would work.
rules.exceptions: Audit trail needed = load-save OK with comment.

rules.name: Database index awareness
rules.check: Suggest indexes for unindexed Where/OrderBy on frequent tables.

rules.name: Select projections
rules.check: Query handlers loading full entity but mapping subset → suggest .Select(e => new Dto(...)).

rules.name: Every handler has tests
rules.check: Verify test file exists for each handler.

rules.name: Test naming convention
rules.check: MethodName_Scenario_ExpectedResult pattern. Flag generic names.

rules.name: Flag hard-to-test handlers
rules.check: Flag >5-6 constructor params, hidden side effects.

rules.name: Track known test gaps
rules.check: Documented gaps must have issue references.

rules.name: Line coverage above 70%
rules.check: Run dotnet test with XPlat Code Coverage. Parse Cobertura XML. Flag if overall or per-project line-rate < 0.70.

rules.name: No console/debug artifacts
rules.check: Flag Console.Write*, Debug.Write*, Debugger.Break/Launch, Trace.Write.

rules.name: No commented-out code
rules.check: Flag 3+ consecutive commented code lines.

rules.name: No TODO/FIXME/HACK
rules.check: Flag TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX, TEMP, WORKAROUND.

rules.name: No unused imports/dead code
rules.check: Flag unused usings, variables, private methods, unreachable code.

rules.name: Collection expressions over ToList/ToArray
rules.check: Flag .ToList() and .ToArray() when assigning to a collection property or variable that can accept a collection expression. Use C# 12 collection expressions: [.. source] instead. Flag new List<T> { items } when [items] works.
rules.why: IDE0305. Collection expressions are more concise and let the compiler choose the optimal collection type.

rules.name: No redundant null-coalescing for defaulted properties
rules.check: When assigning to an entity property that already has a default initializer (e.g., List<string> Technologies { get; set; } = []), flag null-coalescing fallback to the same default (e.g., Technologies = input.Technologies ?? []). If the input is null, the entity's default already handles it — just assign directly or skip the property.
rules.why: Redundant defensive code. The entity default initializer already provides the fallback value, making ?? [] dead logic that obscures intent.

rules.name: ARCHITECTURE.md for significant modules
rules.check: Backend layer directories (Domain, Application, Api, Migrations), feature directories (Application/{Feature}), and module directories (Api/Auth, Api/GitProxy) with 3+ source files must have an ARCHITECTURE.md. Flag directories without one.
rules.why: Embedded architecture docs guide feature developers and code reviewers. Without them, architectural decisions are implicit and easily violated.

rules.name: GUIDELINES.md for significant modules
rules.check: Same directories as DOC-001 must have a GUIDELINES.md with coding patterns, conventions, and dos/don'ts specific to that module.
rules.why: Guidelines docs prevent pattern drift and give new contributors concrete examples to follow.

rules.name: ARCHITECTURE.md minimum sections
rules.check: Existing ARCHITECTURE.md files must have at minimum: a heading with module name, a Purpose section, a Components/Structure section, and a Key Decisions section.
rules.why: Skeleton docs with no content are worse than no docs — they create false confidence.

rules.name: GUIDELINES.md minimum sections
rules.check: Existing GUIDELINES.md files must have at minimum: a heading with module name, a Patterns section (what to follow), and a Conventions section (naming, structure).
rules.why: Guidelines must be actionable. A file with only a heading provides no value.
